🇰🇼 🇺🇸 ⚡️ Kuwaiti-American journalist Ahmed Shihab-Eldin has been locked up in Kuwait for 6 weeks, just for posting a video of a crashed U.S. fighter jet. He shared verified footage of the friendly-fire incident back in early March. Now he’s facing vague charges like “harming national security” and “spreading false info.”

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: Fwd from @ 📝 What Happened? 📝 On the Strait of Hormuz Blockade Despite announced blockade and control of the Strait of Hormuz by Donald Trump , the first results contradict expectations . Monitoring shows at least five vessels have passed through. 🔻 Whose Vessels Passed? ▪️ Confirmed: Rich Starry (China), Murlikishan (Madagascar), Peace Gulf (Panama) and Elpis (Comoros), and the bulk carrier Christianna (Liberia). Some previously faced US sanctions and worked with Iranian cargo. ▪️ Rich Starry, a Chinese vessel, departed UAE and entered the Gulf of Oman. ▪️ Peace Gulf is heading to UAE, Murlikishan to Iraq, not Iranian ports. ▪️ Elpis and Christianna departed Iranian ports after the blockade and passed through unhindered. 🖍 Estimates suggest up to 20 passages in 36 hours. Traffic has not been halted. 🚩 This occurs despite a dense US naval presence in the region, including the aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln . ❗️ Announcing a blockade is easier than enforcing it. Shipowners may take the risk if not calling at Iranian ports. 📌 The "blockade 2.0" seems more a show of presence than an actual closure - traffic continues, rules remain undefined. 📍 High resolution infographic 📍 English version #Iran #USA 🧨 @rybar_mena — on Middle Eastern chaos 💸

WarTranslated "Risk of sarcophagus collapse over Chornobyl Unit 4 after drone strike." - Greenpeace nuclear expert Shaun Burnie. According to Burnie, following a Russian drone strike in February 2025, the structure has lost its airtightness, meaning the radiation containment system is no longer fully functional. In the event of a collapse, radioactive dust could initially rise within the structure and eventually leak outside. https://www.unian.ua/economics/energetics/obval-sarkofagu-nad-4-reaktorom-chaes-faktichno-neminuchiy-scenariy-yaderniy-ekspert-shon-berni-13348179.html tweet

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇺🇸 Republican Senator Lindsey Graham: 📌 “I hope the reports and speculation about another extension of the ceasefire to reach a deal with Iran are unfounded.” 📌 “President Trump’s position is clear to me. I fear the Iranians will once again play their usual game — dragging out time with minor concessions. I believe the key conditions for President Trump are: 1.A complete halt to uranium enrichment; 2.U.S. control over roughly 450 kg of highly enriched uranium; 3.The full opening of the Strait of Hormuz without Iranian interference; 4.Iran abandoning its long-range ballistic missile program and any attempt to develop nuclear weapons; 5.Iran ending support for all terrorist proxy groups in the region. 🇮🇷 “These conditions would allow Iran to exist as a state, but not as the world’s leading sponsor of terrorism. If no deal is reached, it’s time to finish the job.” See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 The IDF said it carried out overnight strikes on multiple Hezbollah infrastructures in the village of Adshit in southern Lebanon. Around 130 rockets have been launched from the village since the start of operations, targeting Israeli forces and areas including Kiryat Shmona. The strikes reportedly targeted weapons depots, launchers, and command centres, and the IDF said Hezbollah fighters were involved in planning attacks against Israel were killed. @wfwitness

Open Source Intel Senator Lindsey Graham: “I hope the speculation and rumors about yet another ceasefire extension to reach a deal with Iran are off base. To me, President Trump’s position is clear. I fear the Iranians will play the same old game, dragging things out with minor, symbolic concessions. I believe the bottom line for President Trump is: • No enrichment • American control of the approximately 900 pounds of highly enriched uranium • The opening of the Strait of Hormuz without interference from Iran • Iran must abandon its long range ballistic missile program and any effort to develop a nuclear weapon • Iran must cease support for all terrorist proxies in the region This would allow Iran to exist as a nation, but not as the world’s largest state sponsor of terrorism. If there is no deal, it is time to finish the job.” tweet

OSINTdefender The U.S.-brokered preliminary talks between Israel and Lebanon on the disarmament of Lebanese Hezbollah (LH) have started at the U.S. State Department in Washington. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, U.S. Ambassador to Lebanon Michel Issa, Counselor Michael Needham, Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter, and Lebanese Ambassador to the U.S. Nada Hamadeh are in attendance. Reciprocal strikes between Israel and LH are ongoing. tweet

OSINTdefender Adm. Brad Cooper, the Commander of U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), has once again arrived in Israel for meetings with Israeli Chief of General Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir and other senior military officials, following so far unsuccessful peace talks with Iran, and the expiration of the current ceasefire on April 21, 2026. While meeting today, Cooper and Zamir were joined by Holocaust survivor Bella Haim in commemoration of Holocaust Remembrance Day. tweet

Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 CNN: The U.S. Navy is not escorting commercial vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, but is informing merchant ships not calling at Iranian ports that it is safe to transit and encouraging passage. A U.S. official clarified that Washington is not enforcing a full blockade of the strait, but rather targeting vessels entering or leaving Iranian ports along the country’s coastline. U.S. destroyers were also sent through the waterway last weekend to demonstrate that a safe navigation route remains open. tweet

OSINTdefender According to Reuters, citing two Trump administration officials, the Trump administration will let the 30-day waiver on the delivery, sale, and offloading of Iranian-origin crude oil and petroleum products expire without renewing it. The waiver, implemented on March 20, 2026, is set to expire at 12:01 a.m. EST on April 19, 2026, amidst the U.S. naval blockade of vessels entering or exiting Iranian ports through the Strait of Hormuz. tweet
